title: Futures 🎁
description: Empty boxes that promise to contain data... eventually.

slides.title: The Magic Box 🎁
slides.content:
# The Promise of Data

If Dart instantly skips past a 3-second download... what does that function actually return on Line 2?

It returns a **`Future`**!
A Future is literally a wrapped gift box. It's empty right now, but it carries a strict promise: *"I will eventually open up and contain your data."*

slides.title: Future Types 🔖
slides.content:
## Tagging the Box

Just like Lists `<int>`, Futures must declare what they will eventually become.

`Future<String>` = "This is an empty box right now, but in a few seconds, it will burst open and hand you a String!"
`Future<void>` = "This will finish running eventually, but it won't hand you any data back."

quizData.question: What does `Future<int>` mathematically represent?

options.text: An integer that is available instantly.
An 'empty box' object that will eventually, at some point in the future, resolve into a usable integer.

quizData.explanation: A Future is exactly what it sounds like: a promise of data arriving in the future.

slides.title: We Can't Touch It! 🚫

quizData.question:
Fill in the exact spelling of the keyword used to represent delayed data:
_______<String> data = fetch();
quizData.explanation: Dart uses the 'Future' object to represent asynchronous tasks.
